author: omerfirmak
content: Looks like full BALs are part of zkevm fixtures, but are they expected to be avaiable to guest programs too? They don't really need them but it might be easier for guests to verify them if the full BAL is available.

author: jsign
content: Since blockAccessListHash is part of the EL header, then more than "easier" I think is unavoidable that the guest program must validate it (even if not used a priori for performance).

author: omerfirmak
content: I think it is avoidable, no? guests can generate BALs themselves during execution and check it against the block header.

author: omerfirmak
content: But it kinda makes the allocation patterns of EVM worse, so I'm not sure if it is worth trying to avoid having them in the input.

author: jsign
content: Ahh you mean removing it from the execution payload. Yeah, I think ideally we would like to use the exact same defs as consensus-specs; but i agree a priori those bytes aren't useful per se.

author: jsign
content: It also helps a bit the ELs that are stateful to become stateless without changing things too much. But I think this doesn't fully apply to other guest programs like yours that are fully designed to be stateless from day zero.

author: omerfirmak
content:
> I think ideally we would like to use the exact same defs as consensus-specs

I guess the idea with this is to make the testing easier, right?

author: omerfirmak
content:
> But I think this doesn't fully apply to other guest programs like yours that are fully designed to be stateless from day zero.

Yeah, I am trying to make it as generic as possible but I'm leaning more towards stateless-friendliness most of the time.

author: jsign
content: I think is more like making CL lifes easier while veirfying the public inputs. Since if we now invent new ExecutionPaylads sturcts, that can create some complexity on the CL side on what new payload request hash tree root values are compard to "unchanged" things
